step_by_step_reasoning: The transcript describes decomposing a monolithic data architecture into purpose-built AWS storage services based on data type. The OutSystems Platform routes different data classifications to S3, Aurora, DynamoDB, and Secrets Manager. KMS provides tenant-isolated encryption across all these storage layers.

graph.name: OutSystems Monolith Data Decomposition
graph.notes: Architectural shift from a single relational database catch-all to purpose-built data stores (S3, Aurora Serverless, DynamoDB, Secrets Manager) with KMS-driven multi-tenant isolation.

nodes.name: OutSystems Platform
nodes.notes: Decomposed application platform routing data based on type.

nodes.name: Amazon S3
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores binary and unstructured blob data.

nodes.name: Aurora Serverless
nodes.notes: Stores core relational definitions with tenant-specific instances.

nodes.name: Amazon DynamoDB
nodes.notes: Stores configuration and key-value pair data.

nodes.name: Secrets Manager
nodes.notes: Stores encrypted sensitive configuration and secrets directly.

nodes.name: AWS KMS
nodes.notes: Manages tenant-specific keys for encryption and BYOK support.

edges.notes: Routes unstructured binary data to S3.
Routes relational configuration to Aurora Serverless.
Offloads key-value configuration data to DynamoDB.
Saves sensitive encrypted data directly into Secrets Manager.
Encrypts S3 storage with tenant-specific keys.
Encrypts Aurora databases with tenant-specific keys.
Encrypts DynamoDB tables with tenant-specific keys.
Encrypts Secrets Manager parameters with tenant-specific keys.
